title: Congo DR squad announcement | FIFA World Cup 2026
original_text:
Congo DR coach Sebastien Desabre has chosen his 26-man squad ahead of the tournament in Canada, Mexico and USA.
Congo DR have announced their 26-man squad for the FIFA World Cup 2026™. Head coach Sebastien Desabre, who guided the Leopards to their first berth to the global finals since 1974, reflected on their path to the tournament, which included a nervy 1-0 win over Jamaica in the FIFA Play-Off Tournament final, and assured Congo DR fans that they will be giving everything they have in North America.
“It has not been simple, but we showed resilience to reach our goal”, said Desabre. “We are proud to be going to the World Cup. There’s also a strong sense of responsibility to make a good showing of ourselves and of our country. Thanks to our Congolese fans who followed us all through our campaign. We will give our all to make everybody proud and show the best image of Congo Democratic Republic.”
There are few surprises in the squad, as Desabre will be relying on almost all the players who earned their World Cup spot in Mexico a month and a half ago. Among the star players in the fold are captain Cedric Bakambu, Chancel Mbemba and Fiston Mayele.
Explore hospitality packages for Congo DR's FIFA World Cup 2026™ matchesExplore hospitality packages for Congo DR's FIFA World Cup 2026™ matches
One of the highlighted inclusions for the Leopards is Gael Kakuta, who hadn’t taken part in the African qualifiers for the World Cup nor worn the Congo DR jersey since 2024, apart from two matches at the last AFCON. Gedeon Kalulu also makes a worthy return, having just come back from a major knee injury.
A few days after the squad was announced, Hibernian FC defender Rocky Bushiri had to withdraw due to injury. He is replaced by Aaron Tshibola, who plays for fellow Scottish team Kilmarnock.
GoalkeepersGoalkeepers
Lionel Mpasi Thimothy Fayulu Matthieu Epolo
DefendersDefenders
Chancel Mbemba Aaron Wan-Bissaka Alex Tuanzebe Arthur Masuaku Joris Kayembe Steve Kapuadi Aaron Tshibola Dylan Batubinsika Gédéon Kalulu
MidfieldersMidfielders
Noah Sadiki Samuel Moutoussamy Edo Kayembe Ngal'ayel Mukau Charles Pickel Nathanael Mbuku Brian Cipenga Meschack Elia Gael Kakuta
ForwardsForwards
Theo Bongonda Fiston Mayele Cedric Bakambu Simon Banza Yoane Wissa
All squads are provisional until the final list of 26 players is announced by FIFA, following their submission by participating teams, on 2 June.
17 June: Portugal v Congo DR - Houston Stadium
23 June: Colombia v Congo DR - Guadalajara Stadium
27 June: Congo DR v Uzbekistan - Atlanta Stadium
